title: Counselling
text:
Counselling - SupportGoWhere
Counselling
Share link
Provides counselling and psychotherapy to individuals, couples and families facing interpersonal, intrapersonal and mental health issues.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based, Home-based, Online
Fees payable
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Counselling and therapy services include:

Family Therapy

Couple/Marital Therapy

Individual Psychotherapy

Pre-marital counselling and marriage preparation

Counselling on sexuality and sexual issues

Counselling and therapy sessions can be conducted in-person or online via Zoom.




Helplines:

Care Corner Counselling Centre also runs a toll-free Mandarin Counselling Hotline at 1800 353 5800 daily from 10am to 10pm, excluding public holidays. 

SAMH Insight Centre also provides a toll-free helpline at 1800 287 7019 (9am to 6pm on weekdays, except 1pm to 2pm and public holidays).

Who is this for?

Individuals, couples and families with issues such as:

Marital and relationship issues

Family and parenting issues

Grief and loss

Mental health

Personal challenges

Stress

Trauma

What to expect?

The service provider will assess the request for counselling and ask your presenting issues and preferred day/time for the counselling and arrange a slot for you accordingly.

The staff will also provide information on the fees.

Counselling intake and assessment is conducted at the first appointment and the client will see the same counsellor for the subsequent sessions until the case is closed or transferred.

If counselling is not suitable, the counsellor may refer you to other appropriate services.

A counselling session takes around 1 hour.

Most issues will need more than 1 counselling session to resolve.

Fees payable for all service providers except SAMH Insight Centre.

How to apply?

For enquiries or appointment booking, call or email the respective service provider.

Information last updated on 12 Sep 2025
